claimText: Extreme Nuclear Transients (ENTs) occur when stars with masses at least three times greater than the Sun are torn apart by supermassive black holes.
evidenceExcerpt: occur when stars with masses at least three times greater than that of the sun are torn apart by supermassive black holes.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and conditions of ENTs.
distractorHooks: Torn apart by neutron stars instead of supermassive black holes

claimText: During an Extreme Nuclear Transient, extreme tidal forces near a black hole's event horizon stretch and compress a star into a long, thin spaghetti-like shape.
evidenceExcerpt: As a star nears a black hole's event horizon, extreme tidal forces stretch and compress the star into a long, thin spaghetti-like shape
examUse: Statement-based question on the mechanism of tidal disruption by black holes.
distractorHooks: Compression into a dense sphere instead of stretching

claimText: The disruption of a massive star by a supermassive black hole during an ENT releases enormous amounts of electromagnetic energy.
evidenceExcerpt: releasing enormous amounts of electromagnetic energy.
examUse: Statement-based question on the observable emissions of ENTs.
